text:
CT ABDOMEN AND PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST
ANNUAL SURVEILLANCE IMAGING

CLINICAL INDICATION: 53-year-old female with history of FIGO stage IIIC high-grade serous ovarian carcinoma, status post primary debulking surgery (total abdominal hysterectomy, bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy, omentectomy, lymph node dissection, peritoneal stripping) with R0 resection achieved, followed by six cycles of carboplatin/paclitaxel chemotherapy (completed approximately 12 months prior). Currently on maintenance niraparib 150 mg daily. Germline BRCA1 pathogenic variant (5382insC) carrier. Presenting for annual surveillance CT. CA-125 12 U/mL (drawn at time of this study).

COMPARISON: CT abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed approximately 6 months prior, and prior CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with PET-CT performed at end of chemotherapy.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT of the abdomen and pelvis was performed in the portal venous phase following the administration of 100 mL of Omnipaque 350 IV contrast without complications. Oral contrast was not administered. Axial images were obtained at 2.5 mm slice thickness with coronal and sagittal reformats reviewed.

---

FINDINGS:

CHEST (imaged on scout/upper cuts):
The lung bases are clear. No pleural effusion. No pleural nodularity. The imaged cardiac silhouette appears unremarkable.

LIVER:
The liver is normal in size and attenuation. No focal hepatic lesions are identified. No surface nodularity or implants. The portal and hepatic veins are patent. Intrahepatic bile ducts are not dilated.

GALLBLADDER AND BILIARY SYSTEM:
Gallbladder is present and unremarkable. No gallstones. Common bile duct is not dilated (3 mm).

PANCREAS:
The pancreas is normal in contour and attenuation throughout the head, body, and tail. No pancreatic ductal dilation. No peripancreatic stranding or fluid.

SPLEEN:
The spleen is normal in size (9.1 cm in craniocaudal dimension) and homogeneous in attenuation. No focal lesions.

ADRENAL GLANDS:
Bilateral adrenal glands are normal in morphology and size. No adrenal masses.

KIDNEYS AND URETERS:
Bilateral kidneys are symmetric in size and demonstrate normal cortical thickness. No hydronephrosis. No renal calculi. No perirenal stranding. No obstructing ureteral lesions.

STOMACH AND SMALL BOWEL:
No bowel wall thickening or obstruction. No mesenteric mass. Bowel loops are decompressed and course normally. No internal hernia.

COLON AND APPENDIX:
No appendix is identified, consistent with prior appendectomy. Colon is unremarkable without wall thickening, mass, or pericolonic stranding. The paracolic gutters are clear bilaterally — no nodules or fluid collections.

PERITONEUM AND OMENTUM:
The peritoneal surfaces throughout the upper and lower abdomen are smooth. No peritoneal nodularity, soft tissue thickening, or implants are seen. The omental region is surgically absent (post-omentectomy). No ascites. No loculated fluid collections. The right and left subphrenic spaces are clear.

DIAPHRAGM:
No diaphragmatic nodularity or thickening. No subphrenic implants. The diaphragmatic contours are smooth bilaterally, without focal soft tissue along the previously described areas of peritoneal stripping.

LYMPH NODES:
No pathologically enlarged lymph nodes are identified in the retroperitoneum, mesentery, para-aortic, paracaval, aortocaval, common iliac, external iliac, internal iliac, or inguinal regions. The previously noted enlarged pelvic and para-aortic nodes are no longer apparent — all nodal stations appear normal in size and morphology.

PELVIS:
Post-surgical changes are again noted, consistent with prior total abdominal hysterectomy and bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy. The vaginal cuff appears well-healed without evidence of soft tissue thickening or mass at the cuff. No adnexal masses (expected given prior BSO). The rectovesical space and retrovesical soft tissues are unremarkable. Bladder is partially distended and smooth-walled. No pelvic sidewall adenopathy. No pelvic free fluid. The previously described peritoneal implants along the pelvic sidewall and cul-de-sac are no longer evident. Surgical clip material is identified at the pelvic sidewall, consistent with prior lymph node dissection sites — no surrounding soft tissue concerning for recurrence.

VASCULAR:
Aorta is normal caliber throughout. No aneurysm. Iliac vessels are patent. No vascular abnormality.

BONES AND SOFT TISSUES:
No suspicious osseous lesions. No lytic or sclerotic foci identified. Soft tissues of the abdominal wall and flanks are unremarkable. A midline anterior abdominal wall scar is noted, consistent with prior laparotomy.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. No evidence of peritoneal carcinomatosis, ascites, adenopathy, or intra-abdominal/pelvic soft tissue masses to suggest disease recurrence. The abdomen and pelvis appear clean on this surveillance study.

2. Post-surgical changes are stable and unchanged compared with the prior examination six months ago — including post-hysterectomy/BSO pelvic anatomy, absent appendix, absent omentum, and pelvic sidewall surgical clips.

3. No liver surface implants or parenchymal lesions. No diaphragmatic nodularity.

4. The visualized lung bases are clear without pleural disease.

5. Overall findings are reassuring and consistent with continued remission. No new or suspicious findings identified.

Study reviewed and reported by: Abdominal Imaging, Department of Radiology.
